title: Time related words
content: Time related words
intro: Time words in Marathi. Use सकाळी/दुपारी/संध्याकाळी/रात्री for "at" time. For a.m./p.m., say morning/afternoon/evening/night explicitly.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: He comes back from school in the evening
We work from 10 am to 10 pm
आम्ही सकाळी 10 ते रात्री 10 काम करतो
AmhI sakALI 10 te rAtrI 10 kAm karato

blocks.heading: Hindu months
blocks.content: चैत्र (chaitr), वैशाख (vaishAkh), ज्येष्ठ (jyeShTh), आषाढ (AShADh), श्रावण (shrAvaN), भाद्रपद (bhAdrapad), अश्विन (ashvin), कार्तिक (kArtik), मार्गशीर्ष (mArgashIrSh), पौष (pauSh), माघ (mAgh), फाल्गुन (phAlgun).

blocks.heading: Telling date
blocks.content: Date = तारीख (tArIkh) (feminine). Use actual numbers (एक, दोन...) not first/second.

title: Tips to Learn Marathi मराठी भाषा सिखनेके नुस्खे marathi bhasha sikhaneke nuskhe
content:
व्याकरण सीखो
शुरुआत में मराठी खबरोंके टीव्ही चेनल देखो
मराठी समाचार पत्र पढो
मराठी शब्दकोश (डिक्शनरी) खरीद लो. और पढते समय उसका इस्तमाल करो
बादमें मराठी टीव्ही सिरिअल्स ( डेली-सोप्स) देखना शुरू करो. शुरुआत में मराठी मूव्ही समझना कठीन हो सकता है. इसलिये सिरिअल्स समझने से शुरुआत करना अच्छा होगा.
बादमें मराठी पत्रिका ( मॅगझिन्स ) पढो
मराठी बोलनेका प्रयस करो. जब तक कोशिश नही करोगे तब तक आत्मविश्वास नही बढेगा.
अभ्याससेही आदमी परिपूर्ण बनाता है
मराठी भाषा कि कईं सारी बोली-भाषाए है. इसलिये प्रथम प्रमाण मराठी पे ध्यान दो
For detailed tips to Learn Marathi (rather any new language) refer my post on “Learn Marathi from English” blog for tips:
Tips to Learn Marathi (rather any new language)

title: Tips to Learn Marathi (rather any new language)
content:
Many users of this blog asked me about tips to Learn Marathi fast. So I would like to share my thoughts about it. These are the things which I followed when I learned Tamil and Gujarati myself. I learned the basics of these languages through the book
Learn Tamil through Marathi by Balaji publication
Rapidex Tamil-Hindi learning course
and then developed myself without anybody’s personal help.
So these are proven methods which will help you to learn not only Marathi but any new language.
You can surely try it.
Learn Grammar :
Read the blog at least once so that you will get to know different grammar rules. You may not remember everything but concepts will be at the back of your mind. As and when you keep reading the blog further or reading Marathi text elsewhere you will be able to relate concepts you had read before. When you will frequently come across the use of these grammar rules, you will remember it. Learning grammar alone can make you an independent speaker. No matter how many ready-made sentences you read, until you understand the rule behind it you will not be able to form sentences yourself. Few people do not like to study grammar, but they keep listening/reading the language and sense the grammar. This is the same way a baby learns the mother tongue without formally knowing grammar rules. Whatever approach suits you; but learn grammar.
Watch Marathi news channel:
Read or listen to the news in any language you know, and then watch the same news in Marathi. As you already know the news, listening to it again in Marathi will be easy to understand. You can relate the words. Thus learn new words. In news channels formal Marathi language is used so it will be easy to understand.
Start reading newspaper:
It has the same benefit as watching the news channel. It has formal language and news knowing you can relate to text faster.
Buy dictionaries:
Buy 2 dictionaries
English-Marathi and Marathi-English.
This will be helpful while reading newspaper. Without dictionaries you can not increase your vocabulary.Dictionaries are available online as well.
Then start watching Marathi serials ( daily-soaps ) :
Then start viewing daily Marathi serials on Marathi channels. These serials have conversations happening in your day to day life. This is very useful. Go for serious nature programs.
Do not try Marathi movies initially
.
Also Do not go for comedy serials/dramas initially
. To understand comedy, one needs to have very good knowledge of the language (hidden meanings, double meanings, etc. ) and social context. So comedy serials/movies will not help you initially. Rather it will discourage you as you may not be able to enjoy it.
Start reading Marathi magazines
Unless you dare to speak you can not be confident:
Wherever possible speak in Marathi with whatever knowledge you have. Ask another person to rectify you. Understand mistake and try again. Keep trying, and one day you will realize that you are speaking without any mistake.
Practice makes man perfect:
As applicable in any aspect of life, this rule is valid in the language learning paradigm as well.
Read more to increase vocabulary.
Listen more to increase understanding capacity.
Talk more to make you confident
. While reading you follow your own pace. You will have time to read the sentence, understand it, check new words and thus increase vocabulary. Watching/Listening Marathi helps you increase your capacity to understand others but while listening to others we tend to gloss over the details i.e. new words.
So two things help in two different ways
Remember Marathi and other Indian languages have dialects. So focus on standard Marathi first :
I think in English only pronunciation of differs depending on region or country but basic grammar rules do not change.
But in India Marathi and all other Indian languages have dialects. Each dialect can have little different grammar rules for verb formation. Each dialect can have a different set of words. That is why you feel the native Marathi speaker sounds different than what you have learned.
Do not get confused. Focus on standard Marathi. Be confident in it then you will be able to relate the dialect and understand it correctly. Even native speaker of one Marathi dialect generally is not able to speak other dialect, though he understands it.
So be confident in Standard Marathi, then try just understanding other dialect and you may continue using your dialect( that is standard Marathi )

title: To ask meaning इसका मतलब क्या-isakA matalab kyA
content:
In Hindi we ask “इसका मतलब क्या”(isakA matalab kyA) ?
“मतलब” in Hindi is अर्थ(arth) in Marathi.
इसका मतलब क्या(isakA matalab kyA) ?
त्याचा अर्थ काय (tyAchA arth kAy) ?
उस सूचना फलक का मतलब क्या ( us sUchanA phalak kA matalab kyA)
त्या पाटीचा अर्थ काय ( tyA pATIchA arth kAy )
उसके भाषणका मतलब क्या था ( usake bhAShaNakA matalab kyA thA)
त्याच्या भाषणाचा अर्थ काय होता (tyAchyA bhAShaNAchA arth kAy hotA)
मेरे बोलने का यह मतलब नहीं था ( mere bolane kA yah matalab nahIM thA)
माझ्या बोलण्याचा अर्थ हा नव्हता (mAjhyA bolaNyAchA arth hA navhatA)

title: To emphasize using-च(ch)-in Marathi-मैने ही-तूने ही maine hI-tUne hI etc.
content:
In Hindi, we emphasize word by appending ही(hI) to it. e.g. मैने ही (maine hI), तूने ही(tUne hI), कल ही (kal hI)
In Marathi, we use च(ch) to emphasize on the word. च(ch) is a one-size-fits-all kind of suffix. You can add it कर्ता (kartA) i.e. doer of an action, कर्म (karm) noun, verb or any other word which you want to emphasize.

title: To emphasize word using-च(ch)-in Marathi
content:
Consider the sentence “I will finish it tomorrow”. Now if you want to emphasize the time you will say “I will finish it tomorrow itself”.
Consider the sentence “He told me that he got a lottery”. Now to emphasize who told you, you will say “He himself told me he got lottery”.
Sometimes Indians tend to create such sentences as “He only told me”, “You only asked me close door” etc.
In Marathi, we use च(ch) to emphasize on the word. च(ch) is a one-size-fits-all kind of suffix. You can add it कर्ता (kartA) i.e. doer of the action, कर्म (karm) noun, verb or any other word which you want to emphasize.

title: To force to do some action-बुलवाऊंगा-करवाएगा-हसवाया-bulavAUMgA-karavAegA -hasavAyA
content:
A Hindi speaking police may say about a thief मै उससे सच बुलवाऊंगा(mai usase sach bulavAUMgA).
A servant may complain about his master as वह मेरेसे काम करवाएगा (vah merese kAm karavAegA )
So here one person is forcing another person to take some action.
In English, we use sentences like “I will make him work”, “That joke made him laugh” etc.
In Marathi such senteces are said as मी करायला लावीन ( mI karAyalA lAvIn ), मी बोलायला लावीन( mI bolAyalA lAvIn ). etc.

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation
content:
To indicate possession
of something we say “I have a car”, “I have a bungalow” etc.
In Marathi, such sentences are formed as follows :
सामन्यरूप(sAmanyarUp)
form of my/his/ ‘s etc.
जवळ(javaL)/ कडे(kaDe )
noun
form of “to be” i.e. असणे (asaNe) as per tense
and gender, multiplicity of noun
माझ्या (mAjhyA)
जवळ(javaL)/ कडे(kaDe )
गाडी (gADI)
आहे (Ahe)
I have a car -> माझ्या जवळ/कडे गाडी आहे (mAjhyA javaL/kaDe gADIAhe)
I have 10 cars -> माझ्या जवळ/कडे १० गाड्या आहेत (mAjhyA javaL/kaDe 10 gADyA Ahet)
I had a car -> माझ्या जवळ/कडे गाडी होती (mAjhyA javaL/kaDe hotI )
I had 10 cars -> माझ्या जवळ/कडे १० गाड्या होत्या(mAjhyA javaL/kaDe 10 gADyA  hotyA)
Anup will have watch -> अनुप जवळ/कडे घड्याळ असेल (anup javaL/kaDe ghaDyAL asel)
I do not have car ->  माझ्या जवळ/कडे गाडी नाही (mAjhyA javaL/kaDe nAhI)
I did not have car ->  माझ्या जवळ/कडे गाडी नव्हती (mAjhyA javaL/kaDe navhatI)

title: To indicate uncertainty-शायद जाऊंगा-शायद करूंगा-shAyad jAUMgA shAyad karUMgA
content:
To indicate uncertainty in Hindi we say. शायद मै जाऊंगा (shAyad mai jAUMgA , शायद वह ये काम करेगा ( shAyad vah ye kAm karegA ).
In Marathi, the sentence is formed similarly as in Hindi. i.e. Sentence is formed in the simple future tense, and a word like
कदाचित (kadAchit), एखादवेळेस (ekhAdaveLes)
etc. is said to indicate uncertainty.

title: “To keep doing” करते रहना, बोलते रहना - karate rahanA, bolate rahanA
content:
In Hindi, we say, मै करते रहता हूं(mai karate rahatA hUM), वह गाते रहता है(vah gAte rahatA hai ). By saying so we indicate the continuity of action. In English, we use “To keep doing” form of a sentence.
e.g I keep trying even if I fail.
He kept speaking until all slept.
Do not stop, keep doing
In Marathi sentence formation is:
Doer of action
verb+ त (t)
form of “राहणे” (rAhaNe) as per subject and tense
मी
बोलत
राहिलो
Like few exceptions the verb “राह” changes to “राहि” before creating its simple past tense.

title: To Know पता होना patA honA e.g. नाम पता है nAm patA hai
content:
In Hindi we say मुझे उसका नाम पता है ( mujhe usakA nAm patA hai ), उसे दुश्मनोंका प्लान पता था ( use dushmanoMkA plAn patA thA).
In Marathi we use verb “माहीत असणे”( mAhIt asaNe ). Sentence formation is similar to Hindi. And as expected verb “माहीत असणे” will be formed as per gender and multiplicity of object.
मुझे तुम्हारा नाम पता है ( mujhe tumhArA nAm patA hai )
तुझे नाव मला माहीत आहे (tujhe nAv malA mAhIt Ahe)
उसे(स्त्री.) उसके(पु.) घरका पता पता है (use(strI.) usake(pu.) gharakA patA patA hai)
तीला त्याच्या घराचा पत्ता माहीत आहे (tIlA tyAchyA gharAchA pattA mAhIt Ahe)
उसे(स्त्री.) उसके(पु.) घरका पता पता था (use(strI.) usake(pu.) gharakA patA patA thA)
तीला त्याच्या घराचा पत्ता माहीत होता(tIlA tyAchyA gharAchA pattA mAhIt hotA)
क्या तुझे ये पता है ( kyA tujhe ye patA hai )
तुला हे माहीत आहे का ? (tulA he mAhIt Ahe kA ?)
क्या उसे यह पता होगा ( kyA use yah patA hogA )
तीला हे माहीत असेल का ? (tIlA he mAhIt asel kA ?)
In Hindi we also use verb पता चलना ( patA chalanA ). For this In Marathi we use verb कळणे ( kaLaNe ) or समजणे (samajaNe ).
For more details, refer
samajhanA patA chalanA समझना पता चलना

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know"
content: To Understand & To come to know
intro: To Understand and To come to know use समजणे (samajaNe) or कळणे (kaLaNe). Structure same as माहीत असणे: Subject (To) + object + verb as per object.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: She came to know the news
He will not come to know this

title: To want-मुझे-चाहिये-नहीं-चाहिये-mujhe-chAhiye-nahIM-chAhiye-etc.
content:
In Hindi, we say मुझे आम चाहिये(mujhe Am chAhiye), उसे किताब चाहिये थी(use kitAb chAhiye thI)
For such use of Hindi verb “चाहना”(chAhanA ), Marathi verb is “पाहिजे असणे (pAhije asaNe)/ हवे असणे (have asaNe).
Sentence formation is in Marathi is same as in Hindi.
In Hindi while forming a sentence for “चाहिये” we use pronoun forms like मुझे, उसे, इसे, कौशिकको etc. which is nothing but the form of noun/pronoun with the preposition “To”
For details of this form refer:
The preposition “TO” in Marathi
Form of subject
object
पाहिजे/ form of हवा as per object
form of  “असणे” as per object
मला
आंबा
पाहिजे/ हवा
आहे
मुझे आम चाहिये(mujhe Am chAhiye)
मला आंबा पाहिजे/हवा आहे (malA AMbA pAhije/havA Ahe)
उसे(पु.) आम चाहिये(use Am chAhiye)
त्याला आंबा पाहिजे/हवा आहे (tyAlA AMbA pAhije/havA Ahe)
उसे(स्त्री.) आम चाहिये(use Am chAhiye)
तीला आंबा पाहिजे/हवा आहे (tIlA AMbA pAhije/havA Ahe )
As you see, the object “mango” is the same in all senescence So verb formation is the same for all sentences i.e.
“पाहिजे आहे / हवा आहे”. Only the form of
a subject
“to me”, ”to him
”,
“to her” are used.
Suppose an object is changed to “mangos” i.e. masculine plural so sentences will be
मुझे/उसे(पु.)/उसे(स्त्री.) दस आम चाहिये(mujhe /use /use das Am chAhiye)
मला / त्याला/ तीला दहा आंबे पाहिजे/हवे आहेत (malA / tyAlA / tIlA AMbe pAhije/have Ahet)
See the verb changed to “हवे”, this is same as we had seen in an article about adjectives (Remember काळा/काळी/काळे ??!!!). Form of supporting verb is changed “आहेत”. This is also same as per the rules of simple present tense.
Just for your reference the form of “हवे” can be summarized as:
एकवचन
बहुवचन
पुल्लिंग
हवा
हवे
स्त्रीलिंग
हवी
हव्या
नपुसकलिंग
हवे
हवी
Go through below examples in different genders. Multiplicity and tenses
Present tense
1) पुल्लिंग e.g. chalk :- खडू (khaDU )
एकवचन
उसे / उन्हे चॉक चाहिये ( use/unhe chok chAhiye )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना खडू
पाहिजे/हवा आहे
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA khaDU pAhije/havA Ahe )
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे दस चॉक चाहिये (use/unhe das ch~ok chAhiye)
त्याला/तीला/त्यांना दहा खडू
पाहिजे/हवे आहेत
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA dahA khaDU pAhije/have Ahet )
2) स्त्रीलिंग e.g पेटी (peTI)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटी चाहिये ( use/unhe peTI chAhiye )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेटी
पाहिजे/हवी आहे
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTI pAhije/havI Ahe )
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटीयां चाहिये (use/unhe peTIyAM chAhiye )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेट्या
पाहिजे/हव्या आहेत
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTyA pAhije/havyA Ahet)
3) नपुसकलिंग e.g. Page :- पान (pAn)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ना चाहिये (use/unhe pannA chAhiye )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पान
पाहिजे/हवे आहे
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAn pAhije/have Ahe )
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ने चाहिये (use/unhe panne chAhiye )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पाने
पाहिजे/हवी आहेत
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAne pAhije/havI Ahet)
Past tense
1) पुल्लिंग
chalk :- खडू (khaDU )
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे चॉक चाहिये था (use/unhe ch~ok chAhiye thA )
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना खडू
पाहिजे/हवा होता
( tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA khaDU pAhije/havA hotA)
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे दस चॉक चाहिये थे (use/unhe das ch~ok chAhiye the)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना खडू
पाहिजे/हवे होते
( tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA khaDU pAhije/have hote)
2) स्त्रीलिंग
Bag :- पेटी (peTI)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटी चाहिये थी ( use/unhe peTI chAhiye thI)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेटी
पाहिजे/हवी होती
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTI pAhije/havI hotI )
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटीयां चाहिये थी (use/unhe peTIyAM chAhiye thI)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेट्या
पाहिजे/हव्या होत्या
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTyA pAhije/havyA hotyA)
3) नपुसकलिंग
Page:- पान (pAn)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ना चाहिये था (use/unhe pannA chAhiye thA)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पान
पाहिजे/हवे होते
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAn pAhije/have hote )
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ने चाहिये थे (use/unhe panne chAhiye the)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पाने
पाहिजे/हवी होती
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAne pAhije/havI hotI)
Future tense
1) पुल्लिंग
chalk:- खडू (khaDU )
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे चॉक चाहिये होगा (use/unhe ch~ok chAhiye hogA)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना खडू
पाहिजे/हवा असेल
( tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA khaDU pAhije/havA asel)
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे दस चॉक चाहिये होंगे (use/unhe das ch~ok chAhiye hoMge)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना खडू
पाहिजे/हवे असतील
( tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA khaDU pAhije/have asatIl)
2) स्त्रीलिंग
Bag :- पेटी (peTI)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटी चाहिये होगी( use/unhe peTI chAhiye hogI)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेटी
पाहिजे/हवी असेल
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTI pAhije/havI asel)
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पेटीयां चाहिये होगी(use/unhe peTIyAM chAhiye hogI)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पेट्या
पाहिजे/हव्या असतील
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA peTyA pAhije/havyA asatIl)
3) नपुसकलिंग
Page:- पान (pAn)
एकवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ना चाहिये होगा (use/unhe pannA chAhiye hogA)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पान
पाहिजे/हवे असेल
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAn pAhije/have asel)
बहुवचन
उसे/उन्हे पन्ने चाहिये होंगे (use/unhe panne chAhiye hoMge)
त्याला/ तीला /त्यांना पाने
पाहिजे/हवी असतील
(tyAlA/tIlA/tyAMnA pAne pAhije/havI asatIl)

title: "To Want" with other verbs जाना है,करना है,नही खाना है-jAnA hai-karanA hai-nahI khAne hai etc.
content:
In Hindi we say
मुझे घर जाना है(mujhe ghar jAnA hai), उसे क्रिकेट देखना था(use krikeT dekhanA thA), मुझे आज काम खतम करना है (mujhe Aj kAm khatam karanA hai), etc.
जाना है,करना है, देखना था indicate a desire for some activity.
In Marathi, such sentence formation is:-
Form of subject with preposition "to"
object
Main verb + form of आयचा(AyachA)
form of  “असणे” as per object and tense
मला
आंबा
खायचा
होता
The form of आयचा (AyachA) is created based on Gender and multiplicity of object; similar to what we have seen in Adjectives.
i.e. it follows
आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
I will summarize it as follows :-
Singular
Plural
Masculine
आयचा ( AyachA )
आयचे (Ayache)
Feminine
आयची (AyachI)
आयच्या (AyachyA)
Neuter
आयचे (Ayache)
आयची (AyachI)

If you do not want to download the software then you can use Google’s transliteration service

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we"
content: Using Let and Shall we
intro: For suggestions: suffix ऊया (UyA) to verb, often start with चला (chalA). For allowing activity: append ऊ दे (U de) to verb. For You(plural) use ऊ द्या (U dyA).

blocks.heading: Using Let for suggestion
blocks.content: Suffix ऊया (UyA) to verb. Start with चला (chalA). आपण can be omitted.

blocks.heading: Negative – Let's not
blocks.content: Add नको (nako) before verb. चला generally not used.

blocks.heading: Shall we – questions
blocks.content: Two ways: 1) Verb+ऊया का (UyA kA). 2) Verb+आयचा/आयची/आयचे etc. + का. Form of आयचा follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule.

blocks.heading: Using Let for allowing activity
blocks.content: Append ऊ दे (U de) to verb. For You(plural) use ऊ द्या (U dyA).

blocks.heading: Negative – Don't let
blocks.content: Use देऊ नकोस (deU nakos) for singular you, देऊ नका (deU nakA) for plural you.

blocks.heading: Don't let – examples
rows: You all, don't let her speak
You all, don't let me eat

title: Using May to express wish
content:
In English we say “May God bless you”, “May he help you”, “May door open soon”. Here we have used “may” express wish.
In Marathi, we say such wishes by appending ओ(o) for singular and ओत(ot) to plural.
e.g.
for verb do i.e. करणे(karaNe) forms are करो(karo) and करोत (karot)
for verb speak i.e. बोलणे(bolaNe) forms are बोलो(bolo) and बोलोत (bolot)
For verbs not ending with अ(a) we append वो(vo) for singular and वोत(vot) for plural.
for verb give i.e.देणे (deNe) forms are देवो ( devo ) and देवोत ( devot )
for verb become i.e होणे ( hoNe ) forms are होवो ( hovo ) and होवोत ( hovot )
May God bless you -> देव तुम्हाला आशिर्वाद देवो ( dev tumhAlA AshirvAd devo )
May he help you -> तो तुम्हाला मदत करो ( to tumhAlA madat karo )
May they help you -> ते तुम्हाला मदत करोत ( te tumhAlA madat karot )
May new year be happy to you -> नवीन वर्ष सुखदायक असो ( navIn varSh sukhadAyak aso )
May shop open soon -> दुकान लवकर उघडो ( dukAn lavakar ughaDo)
Give above is the word to word translation of “May God bless you”, but as custom, in Marathi, we say देव तुमचं भलं करो ( dev tumachaM bhalaM karo )
In Marathi, we generally use this form only for third-person. Not with first or second person i.e. not with मी, आम्ही, तू, तुम्ही.
So In English, we can form a sentence like “May you help me” which will be in Marathi as तू मला मदत करो ( tU malA madat karo ). So though grammatically perfect, it sounds a bit irregular in Marathi.
Wish / To wish
“Wish” (noun) -> इच्छा (ichCA) it is feminine-singular noun. To wish -> इच्छा असणे (ichCA asaNe)
The sentence using इच्छा असणे (ichCA asaNe) is similar to saying “It is my wish that… may/should…”
I wish he goes to school -> It is my wish that he should go to school -> माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की तो शाळेत गेला पाहिजे.(mAjhI ashI ichChA Ahe kI to shALet gelA pAhije.)
He wishes Kaushik wins -> It is his wish that may Kaushik win -> त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की कौशिक जिंको(tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI Kaushik jiMko)
It is his wish that Kaushik should win -> त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की कौशिकने जिंकावे (tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI kaushikane jiMkAve)
With अकर्मक(akarmak) verbs; when expressing wish; we create verb form as per gender and multiplicity of
subject
Subject
verb+ form of "वा" depending on gender and multiplicity of subject i.e.
आवा-आवी-आवे-आवे-आव्या-आवी  (AvA-AvI-Ave-Ave-AvyA-AvI)
तो (to)
जावा (jAvA)
To speak:- बोलणे (bolaNe) Froms will be बोलावा – बोलावी -बोलावे -बोलावे -बोलाव्या -बोलावी (boAvA-boAvI-boAve-boAve-boAvyA-boAvI)
Examples:
I wish that he should win-> माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की तो जिंकावा (mAjhI ashI ichChA Ahe kI to jiMkAvA)
I wish that she should win-> माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की ती जिंकावी (mAjhI ashI ichChA Ahe kI to jiMkAvI)
He wishes that I(boy) win-> त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की मी जिंकावा (tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI mI jiMkAvA)
He wishes that I(girl) win -> त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की मी जिंकावी (tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI mI jiMkAvI)
He wishes that we win -> त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की आम्ही जिंकावे (tyAchI ashI ichChA Ahe kI AmhI jiMkAve)
1) If the verb does not end with sound अ(a) then we simply omit आ(A) and appendवा-वी-वे-वे-व्या-वी (vA-vI-ve-ve-vyA-vI)
To eat:- खाणे(khANe)
खावा-खावी-खावे-खावे-खाव्या-खावी (khAvA-khAvI-khAve-khAve-khAvyA-khAvI)
2) Sometimes extra त (t) is added to the plural form. So both are acceptable forms.
बोलावा – बोलावी -बोलावे -बोलावेत -बोलाव्यात -बोलावीत (boAvA-boAvI-boAve-boAvet-boAvyAt-boAvIt)
खावा-खावी-खावे-खावेत-खाव्यात-खावीत (khAvA-khAvI-khAve-khAvet-khAvyAt-khAvIt)
e.g.
You wish that they should win-> तुझी अशी इच्छा आहे की ते जिंकावे / जिंकावेत (mAjhI ashI ichChA Ahe kI te jiMkAvet)
3) When the subject is You(singular) i.e. तू(tU); extra स(s) is appended to verb form.
I wish that you(boy) should smile -> माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की तू हसावास (mAjhI ashi ichChA Ahe kI tU hasAvAs)
He wishes that you(boy) should cry ->त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की तू रडावास (tyAchI ashi ichChA Ahe kI tU raDAvAs)
I wish that you(girl) should smile ->माझी अशी इच्छा आहे की तू हसावीस (mAjhI ashi ichChA Ahe kI tU hasAvIs)
He wishes that you(girl) should cry ->त्याची अशी इच्छा आहे की तू रडावीस (tyAchI ashi ichChA Ahe kI tU raDAvIs)

title: Using “Must”/“to be have to”
content: Using Must/have to
intro: To indicate mandatory action we use 'must'/'have to' in English. In Marathi: Subject (To) + verb+आयला (AyalA) OR verb+आवे form as per object + form of लागणे (lAgaNe) as per tense and object. Form of आवे follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule: आवा-आवी-आवे-आवे-आव्या-आवी.

blocks.heading: Examples – by tense (neuter object)
rows: He has to do it every day
He will have to do it every day
He had to do it every day

blocks.heading: Examples – by tense (mango)
rows: She will have to eat mango

blocks.heading: अकर्मक verbs
blocks.content: For अकर्मक verbs (no object), use neuter-singular form of verb.

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Marathi
content: Using phrase supposed to
intro: Supposed to is usually said as 'want to'. Alternative: use subject in simple past form (त्याने) instead of 'To' form (त्याला). Structure: Subject (past form) + object + verb+आयचा + असणे. आयचा follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई.

blocks.heading: Style 1 – as want to
rows: I am supposed to meet him tomorrow

blocks.heading: Examples – सकर्मक
rows: I am supposed to finish that bottle
He is supposed to finish that bottle
She is supposed to finish that bottle
I/He/She was supposed to finish that bottle
I/He/She was supposed to finish those bottles

blocks.heading: अकर्मक verbs
blocks.content: For अकर्मक verbs use आयचे (Ayache). असणे can be omitted in spoken language.

blocks.heading: Examples – अकर्मक
rows: I am supposed to go there tomorrow
He is supposed to come tomorrow

blocks.heading: Special uses
rows: We could not meet but next time we must meet
यावेळी भेटू शकलो नाही पण पुढच्या वेळी नक्की भेटायचं
yAveLI bheTU shakalo nAhI paN puDhachyA veLI nakkI bheTAyachM
Do attend my uncle's wedding – chintu, motu, gotu
माझ्या काकाच्या लग्नाला यायचं हं – चिंटू, मोटू, गोटू
mAjhyA kAkAchyA lagnAlA yAyachM haM – chiMTU, moTU, goTU
You must pay me ransom tomorrow itself
तू मला उद्याच्या उद्या खंडणी द्यायची
tU malA udyAchyA udyA khMDaNI dyAyachI

title: Using phrase “to keep doing”
content: Using phrase to keep doing
intro: To indicate continuity of action we use 'To keep doing'. In Marathi: Doer + verb+त (t) + form of राहणे (rAhaNe) as per subject and tense. Verb राह changes to राहि before simple past tense.

title: Using plurals to indicate respect आदरार्थी बहुवचन (Adararthi bahuvachan )
content: Using plurals to indicate respect आदरार्थी बहुवचन (AdarArthI bahuvachan)
intro: In Marathi, plurals are used to indicate respect. This is called आदरार्थी बहुवचन (AdarArthI bahuvachan).

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
While learning Marathi it is important to understand the customs or traditions of the Marathi community which get reflected in language. One important aspect to learn is how elders are addressed in Marathi.

In Marathi, plurals are used to indicate respect. This is called **आदरार्थी बहुवचन** (AdarArthI bahuvachan).

blocks.heading: Example: Prime Minister
blocks.content:
If you are talking about the Prime Minister of your country. In English, you will say "He is Prime Minister. He is speaking".

In Marathi, "He" is तो (to). Literal translation: तो पंतप्रधान आहे. तो बोलत आहे (to paMtapradhAn Ahe. to bolat Ahe)

But because you are talking about P.M. with respect, you use the plural of तो (to) which is ते (te):
ते पंतप्रधान आहेत. ते बोलत आहेत (te paMtapradhAn Ahet. te bolat Ahet)

Literally in English: "They are Prime Minister. They are speaking" — which sounds odd in English, but in Marathi it is the natural and correct way.

blocks.heading: Examples with ती and तू
blocks.content:
**Lady:** She is good → ती चांगली आहे (tI chAMgalI Ahe)
With respect (plural of ती is त्या): त्या चांगल्या आहेत (tyA chAMgalyA Ahet)

**How are you:** तू कसा आहेस (tU kasA Ahes)
With respect (plural of तू is तुम्ही): तुम्ही कसे आहात (tumhI kase AhAt)

blocks.heading: Historical note: आम्ही for self
blocks.content:
In old times, few learned or rich persons used to address themselves with respect, using आम्ही (AmhI) while talking about themselves.

I am coming → मी येतो आहे (mI yeto Ahe)
Giving oneself respect they used: आम्ही येत आहोत (AmhI yet Ahot)

In editorials of Marathi newspapers, you will find the editor calling himself आम्ही. But nowadays in day-to-day communication, nobody calls himself आम्ही as it looks like boasting.

blocks.heading: Respect without pronouns
blocks.content:
Even if sentences do not have pronouns, they should be formed in plural way to indicate respect.

**Examples:**
- Shivaji Maharaj was great
  - Literal: शिवाजी महाराज महान होता (shivAjI mahArAj mahAn hotA)
  - With respect: शिवाजी महाराज महान होते (shivAjI mahArAj mahAn hote)

- Kaushik is my teacher
  - Literal: कौशिक माझा शिक्षक आहे (kaushik mAjhA shikshak Ahe)
  - With respect: कौशिक माझे शिक्षक आहेत (kaushik mAjhe shikshak Ahet)

**Note:** We do not use the plural of the main noun. Only the pronoun, adjective, and verb use plural form.
- Your mother is good (with respect): तुझ्या आई चांगल्या आहेत (tujhyA AI chAMgalyA Ahet) — we use आई, not आया
- She is my husband's sister: त्या माझ्या नणंद आहेत (tyA mAjhyA naNaMd Ahet) — we do not use नणंदा (plural of नणंद)

**But** when you use a preposition with a noun, we use the plural:
- To mother (literal): आईला (AIlA)
- To mother (with respect): आईंना (AIMnA)
- For teacher (literal): शिक्षकासाठी (shikShakAsAThI)
- For teacher (with respect): शिक्षकांसाठी (shikShakAMsAThI)

blocks.heading: Using आपण (ApaN) instead of तुम्ही (tumhI)
blocks.content:
When speaking very formally or in very formal written communication, instead of तुम्ही (tumhI) we use आपण (ApaN). The rest of the sentence structure remains the same.

**Examples:**
- How are you?
  - With respect: तुम्ही कसे आहात (tumhI kase AhAt)
  - Very formal: आपण कसे आहात (ApaN kase AhAt)

- You are doing work
  - With respect: तुम्ही काम करत आहात (tumhI kAm karat AhAt)
  - Very formal: आपण काम करत आहात (ApaN kAm karat AhAt)

- What do you want?
  - With respect: तुम्हाला काय पाहिजे? (tumhAlA kAy pAhije)
  - Very formal: आपल्याला काय पाहिजे? (ApalyAlA kAy pAhije)

title: Using “Should”
content: Using Should
intro: In Marathi 'should' indicates desired behavior. Style 1: Form of noun/pronoun (as in simple past) + verb+आयला (AyalA) + पाहिजे/form of हवे (have) + form of असणे. Form of हवे: हवा-हवी-हवे-हवे-हव्या-हवी (आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule).

blocks.heading: Style 1 – alternative negative with नको
blocks.content: Use नको instead of नाही पाहिजे.

blocks.heading: Style 2
blocks.content: Append पाहिजे (pAhije) to simple past tense sentence. For सकर्मक verbs use पाहिजे; for plural object use पाहिजेत. For अकर्मक verbs use पाहिजे; for third person plural use पाहिजेत. For You(singular): move स(s) from verb to पाहिजे.

blocks.heading: Style 3 – older/bookish (verb+वा form)
blocks.content: Form: Subject (past form) + Object + verb+आवा/आवी/आवे etc. Forms follow आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule. For You(singular) add extra स.

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think" सोचना - लगना in Marathi
content:
सोचना (
sochanA
)
in Hindi is
विचार करणे (
vichAr karaNe
)
in Marathi
“To Think” in English is:
मै गणित के बारे में सोच रहा हू ( mai gaNit ke bAre meM soch rahA hU )
मी गणिताबद्दल विचार करत आहे ( mI gaNitAbaddal vichAr karat Ahe )
वह उच्च शिक्षा के बारे में सोच रही है ( vah uchch shikShA ke bAre meM soch rahI hai )
ती उच्च शिक्षणाचा विचार करत आहे ( tI uchch shikShaNAchA vichAr karat Ahe )
There is another use of verb
लगना(
laganA
)
in Hindi.
e.g.
मुझे ऐसा लगता है ( mujhe aisA lagatA hai), उसे बूरा लगेगा (use bUrA lagegA )
Marathi verb for is
लगना(
laganA
)
is
वाटणे (
vATaNe
)
Sentence structure in Marathi and Hindi is
the same
:
Proper noun or Pronoun with preposition “To”
noun
Form of verb वाटणे(vATaNe)as per tense and  gender, multiplicity of noun
मला malA
असे ase
वाटते vATate

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think"
content:
“To Think” in English is विचार करणे ( vichAr karaNe ) in Marathi
I am thinking about Maths:- मी गणिताबद्दल विचार करत आहे ( mI gaNitAbaddal vichAr karat Ahe )
She is thinking of higher study :- ती उच्च शिक्षणाचा विचार करत आहे ( tI uchch shikShaNAchA vichAr karat Ahe )
There is another use of the verb “to think” or rather the phrase “To think that”. In such sentences “To Think” or “To think that” means “To feel”
In English we say “I feel I should go”, “I felt happy”, “He feels that everyone else is stupid”.
In these sentences, the phrase “To think that” can be used instead of “To feel”.
e.g. “I think that I should go”, “He thinks that everyone else is stupid”.
In Marathi the verb for “To feel” or “To think that” is वाटणे ( vATaNe ).
Sentence structure is:
Proper noun or Pronoun with preposition “To”
noun
Form of verb वाटणे (vATaNe)as per tense and  gender, multiplicity of noun
मला malA
असे ase
वाटते vATate
So sentence structure is Marathi is kind of saying “It is felt to me”

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs
content: Using To Like with other verbs
intro: Subject (To) + object + verb+आयला (AyalA) + form of आवडणे (AvaDaNe) as per object. For person as object use ला/ना and neuter-singular आवडते.

blocks.heading: Examples – neuter
rows: She will like to sing song
She will like to sing songs

blocks.heading: Negative examples
rows: I do not like to eat mango
I do not like to eat mangoes
He did not like to open bag
She will not like to sing song

blocks.heading: Person as object
blocks.content: Use ला/ना with person. Verb always neuter-singular: आवडते.

blocks.heading: Person as object examples
rows: They will like to hold us

title: Using “To Like”
content: Using To Like
intro: To Like (be fond of) in Marathi is आवडणे (AvaDaNe). Verb form is as per object. For similarity use सारखा (sArakhA) – forms follow आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई rule.

blocks.heading: Like (similarity) – सारखा
blocks.content: सारखा–सारखी–सारखे–सारखे–सारख्या–सारखी

blocks.heading: Similarity examples
rows: Do not speak like a fool

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Marathi
content:
In English, we use the verb “To Mean” e.g. “What does it mean”, “What is its meaning”. “I did not mean it” etc.
In Marathi “meaning” is अर्थ(arth).
In Marathi the translation of sentence “what does it mean” is actually similar to translation of “what is its meaning”
1)What does it mean -> What is its meaning -> त्याचा अर्थ काय (tyAchA arth kAy)
2)What does that signboard mean -> What is meaning of that signboard ->त्या पाटीचा अर्थ काय ( tyA pATIchA arth kAy )
3)What did his speech mean -> What was the meaning of his speech -> त्याच्या भाषणाचा अर्थ काय होता (tyAchyA bhAShaNAchA arth kAy hotA)
When you are asking the meaning of some word you can ask it, in short, using म्हणजे काय (mhaNaje kAy ) as follows:
What does “encyclopedia” mean -> “encyclopedia” म्हणजे काय (mhaNaje kAy )
What does “Sundar” mean in Marathi -> मराठीमधे सुंदर म्हणजे काय (marAThImadhe suMdar mhaNaje kAy )
In English, we also say “I did not mean it”
, “He did not mean insulting you”. Such sentences are said bit differently in Marathi.
I did mean it -> My saying meant this -> माझ्या बोलण्याचा अर्थ हा होता (mAjhyA bolaNyAchA arth hA hotA)
I did not mean it -> My saying did not mean this -> माझ्या बोलण्याचा अर्थ हा नव्हता (mAjhyA bolaNyAchA arth hA navhatA)
When we use “To mean” to indicate purpose
, it is said differently in Marathi.
Purpose in Marathi is उद्देश(uddesh)
“He did not mean hurting you” -> His purpose was not to hurt you -> त्याचा उद्देश तुम्हाला दुखवण्याचा नव्हता (tyAchA uddesh tumhAlA dukhavaNyAchA navhatA)
I meant to insult him -> My purpose was to insult him ->माझा उद्देश त्याचा अपमान करण्याचा होता (majhA uddesh tyAchA apamAn karaNyAchA hotA)

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs
content: Using To Want/To Need with other verbs
intro: Subject (To) + object + verb+आयचा/आयची/आयचे + form of असणे. आयचा follows आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई. For person as object use neuter-singular with ला/ना.

blocks.heading: Examples – neuter
rows: She will want to sing song
She will want to sing songs

blocks.heading: Negative examples
rows: I do not want to eat mango
He did not want to open bag
She will not want to sing song

blocks.heading: Object is a person
blocks.content: Use ला/ना with person. Verb always neuter-singular: बघायचे.

title: Using "To Want" with other person
content: Using To Want with other person
intro: I want him to X = To me, he should X. Use 'should' structure: Subject (To) + sentence with पाहिजे.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I want you to finish work
She wants me to avoid junk food
Kaushik wants students to practice more
कौशिकला विद्यार्थ्यांनी जास्त सराव करायला पाहिजे
kaushikalA vidyArthyAMnI jAst sarAv karAyalA pAhije
Kaushik wanted students to practice more
कौशिकला विद्यार्थ्यांनी जास्त सराव करायला पाहिजे होता
kaushikalA vidyArthyAMnI jAst sarAv karAyalA pAhije hotA
Students want Kaushik to talk slowly

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to"
content: Using verb Can/To be able to and जमणे
intro: 'To can' or 'To be able to' in Marathi is शकणे (shakaNe). Sentence formation: Subject + verb+ऊ(U) + form of शकणे (shakaNe) as per tense and subject. In all tenses, form of शकणे is as per subject.

blocks.heading: Can – examples
rows: They (plural of she) will be able to move

blocks.heading: Negative sentences for Can
blocks.content: Structure: Subject + verb+ऊ(U) + negative form of शकणे (shakaNe). e.g. मी खाऊ शकत नाही

blocks.heading: Can not – examples
rows: They will not be able to move

blocks.heading: Verb जमणे (jamaNe)
blocks.content: जमणे (jamaNe) is also used to indicate ability. Structure: Form of subject with 'To' + Object + main verb + आयला (AyalA) + Form of जमणे (जमले/जमते/जमेल).

blocks.heading: जमणे – examples
rows: I will be able to play cricket
He will be able to eat mangoes

blocks.heading: Note: जमणे with object gender
blocks.content: Sometimes form of जमणे changes as per object gender. जमणे also means 'To gather' when subject is not used with preposition 'to'.

blocks.heading: जमणे – form changes as per object

title: Using verb "To Know"
content: Using verb To Know
intro: To know (facts) in Marathi is माहीत असणे (mAhIt asaNe). Structure: Subject (To) + object + माहीत + form of असणे as per object. For knowing ability use येणे (yeNe). For knowing a person use ओळखणे (oLakhaNe).

blocks.heading: Examples – knowing facts
rows: Your name is known to me

blocks.heading: Negative – knowing facts
rows: Your name is not known to me
She does not know his address
She did not know his address

blocks.heading: Knowing ability (येणे)
blocks.content: For 'I know swimming/cooking' etc. use: Subject (To) + object + verb+ता (tA) + form of येणे (yeNe) as per object.

blocks.heading: Past – knowing ability
rows: She used to know dancing before
He learned. So he could speak Japanese
तो शिकला. म्हणून त्याला जपानी बोलता आले
to shikalA. mhaNUn tyAlA japAnI bolatA Ale

blocks.heading: Negative – knowing ability
rows: I do not know eating cake
She did not know dancing before
They did not know to talk
He did not learn. So he could not speak Japanese
तो शिकला नाही. म्हणून त्याला जपानी बोलता आले नाही
to shikalA nAhI. mhaNUn tyAlA japAnI bolatA Ale nAhI

blocks.heading: Knowing a person (ओळखणे)
blocks.content: For 'I know Mr. Ram' use ओळखणे (oLakhaNe). Preposition 'To' with the person referred. Verb form as per subject/doer.

title: Using verb "To remember" in Marathi
content: Using verb To remember
intro: To remember is आठवणे (AThavaNe). Three uses: (1) not forgotten – आठवणे as per object; (2) memory triggered – आठवण येणे; (3) keep in mind – लक्षात ठेवणे.

blocks.heading: Case 1: Not forgotten
blocks.content: Verb आठवणे as per object. Subject with 'To'. Or use लक्षात असणे.

blocks.heading: Case 2: Memory triggered (I miss...)
blocks.content: Use आठवण येणे. Your remembrance comes to me.

blocks.heading: Case 2 examples
rows: She missed her mother a lot
तीला तीच्या आईची खूप आठवण आली
tIlA tIchyA AIchI khUp AThavaN AlI

blocks.heading: Case 3: Keep in mind
blocks.content: Use लक्षात ठेवणे (lakShAt ThevaNe).

blocks.heading: Case 3 examples
rows: त्याने माझा सल्ला नेहमी लक्षात ठेवला
tyAne mAjhA sallA nehamI lakShAt ThevalA

title: Using verb To Want/To Need
content: Using verb To Want/To Need
intro: To Want or To Need in Marathi is पाहिजे असणे (pAhije asaNe) / हवे असणे (have asaNe). Marathi structure is like 'Mango is wanted to me'. Form of subject + Object + पाहिजे/form of हवा as per object + form of असणे as per object.

blocks.heading: Negative sentence
blocks.content: Use नको (nako) instead of पाहिजे/हवा. Structure: Subject + object + नको + form of असणे.

blocks.heading: Alternative negative
blocks.content: Replace पाहिजे with नाही पाहिजे (nAhI pAhije).

blocks.heading: Alternative negative – examples
rows: She does not want ice cream

title: Verb “To Become/To Happen”
content: Verb To Become/To Happen
intro: In Marathi 'To Become' is होणे (hoNe). 'To Happen' is घडणे (ghaDaNe) but normally we use होणे (hoNe) for both. होणे changes to झा (jhA) in simple past tense – DO NOT use होला/होली/होले.

title: Verbs in Marathi
content: Verbs in Marathi
intro: Learn common Marathi verbs. Just saying the verb is asking someone to do that action — e.g. ये (ye) = Come!

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
Friends, let us move a step ahead in learning sentence formation in Marathi. We will talk about verbs in Marathi.

In English, some verbs are: to go; to come; to speak; to sit; to eat, etc.
In Marathi some verbs are: येणे (yeNe), जाणे (jANe), बोलणे (bolaNe), बसणे (basaNe), खाणे (khANe) etc.

In most cases, while using the verb in an English sentence, we strip "to" out of it. e.g. "To come" is used as "I come" etc. The same way in Marathi, the ending णे (Ne) is stripped in the sentence.

Like in English, just saying the verb is asking someone to do that action. When you say "Come!" you are asking someone to come. Same way in Marathi saying "ये (ye)" is asking someone to come. So by knowing the verbs you can easily request someone.

title: Verbs indicating cause of action. Causative verb
content: Verbs indicating cause of action. Causative verb
intro: Causative verb indicates who caused the action. Main verb हलणे (halaNe) -> Causative हलवणे (halavaNe). No fixed rule—learn each pair.

blocks.heading: Concept
blocks.content: हलणे (halaNe) + व(v) -> हलवणे (halavaNe).

blocks.heading: Way 1: Add व (v)
blocks.content: हल (hala) + व(v) -> हलव (halav).

blocks.heading: Way 2: अ(a) to आ(A)
blocks.content: Change अ(a) to आ(A) in the first syllable.

blocks.heading: Way 2 examples
rows: To cause to die / To kill

blocks.heading: Way 3: उट(uT) to ओड(oD)
blocks.content: Change उट(uT) to ओड(oD) in the verb stem.

blocks.heading: Way 4: Unrelated verb
blocks.content: Causative uses a different verb entirely.

blocks.heading: Way 4 examples
rows: To cause to drink / To feed liquid

blocks.heading: Important
blocks.content: No grammar rule—custom dictates. Not all verbs have causative. Treat each verb separately.

blocks.heading: Verbs that look like causative but mean different
rows: To keep animal (not cause to run)

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object
content: Verbs which use preposition To with subject
intro: Some verbs use 'To' with subject and verb form as per object: मिळणे, आठवणे, आवडणे, पाहिजे/हवा, वाटणे, सापडणे, राग येणे, मान्य असणे.

blocks.heading: To get – मिळणे (miLaNe)
blocks.content: I get a prize = To me a prize gets.

blocks.heading: More examples
rows: I found my wallet under the bed

title: Vowels, Consonants in Marathi and their pronunciation
content: Pronunciation conventions for Marathi alphabets. Learn vowels, consonants, and key pronunciation rules.
intro: Pronunciation conventions for Marathi alphabets. Each letter represents a consistent sound. Use the speaker buttons to hear pronunciation.

blocks.heading: Consonant Pronunciation Notes (व्यंजन)
blocks.content: Each consonant combines with a vowel sound to make syllables (e.g., क + अ = क /ka/). Retroflex sounds (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण, ळ) use tongue curled back (harder), represented by capital style in transliteration when needed. Some consonants like ङ /ṅ/ and ञ /ña/ are nasal stops (nose sound) and different from न /na/.

blocks.heading: Key Pronunciation Rules
blocks.content:
**One letter = one sound:** Each Marathi letter generally represents a consistent sound.

**Vowel quality:** इ /i/ is like "i" in sit, ई /ī/ is like "ee" in see. उ /u/ and ऊ /ū/ are like "oo" in foot and cool. ए /e/ is a mid-front sound like ay in say. ऐ /ai/ is a diphthong like ai in time. ओ /o/ is like o in go, औ /au/ is like ow in cow.

**Retroflex vs dental:** t/T, d/D represent retroflex vs dental articulations (hard vs soft).

**Schwa behavior:** The inherent "a" vowel is often not pronounced at the end in real speech (schwa deletion variations).

title: Vowels, Consonants in Marathi and their pronunciation
content:
In this lesson, we will learn the pronunciation of the alphabet in Marathi.
Friends, In the beginning, we will write Marathi in the English script. So as to enable you to read it, we will decide some conventions for it.
Note that in some cases there is some difference in pronunciation when the capital letter is used instead of a small letter. e.g n/N ; t/T ; d/D
Go through it once. You may not remember all pronunciation now. But as and when you will use it in the sentence, come back to these rules and check it. After a few days, you will remember it automatically.
Also, I have mentioned Devanagari symbols. So later when we will start using Devanagari. you can refer this chart to get the sound for each symbol.

title: Welcome to Learn Marathi from Hindi
content:
Learning Marathi through Hindi is very easy. Being conversant with Hindi has many advantages while learning Marathi.
1) Both languages use the same script i.e. Devanagari. So by default, you can read Marathi.
2) The beauty of Devanagari with respect to the Roman script is that the pronunciation is the same as what it is written. So you will not make mistake in pronunciation of Marathi words. So that part is automatically taken care of
3) Mostly the sentence formation rules (e.g. a sequence of the noun, verb etc.) in Marathi is the same as Hindi. So generally you map a Hindi sentence into Marathi word by word.
In short, you know Hindi means 80% you already know Marathi.
So in this coming lessons, we will try to cover up those 20% !!
In my blog for learning Marathi through English(
http://kaushiklele-learnmarathi.blogspot.in
)
I had explained grammar in detail. English is a completely different language, I thought it necessary to introduce a learner with grammar then slowly moving to simple sentences and then big conversations.
But as you know Hindi, I would directly start with Marathi sentences. You will be able to extract the grammar automatically. Wherever required I will explain the difference.
Also, many people who know Hindi want to grasp simple sentences quickly and develop their skills by more listening and reading. For them, learning grammar in detail is a discouraging factor.
So the approach is completely changing while learning Marathi from Hindi.
For some examples transliteration is given in Roman script is as well. This is for the convenience of search-ability on internet.

title: Welcome to world of Marathi
content:
Hello friends, welcome to my blog, “Learn Marathi through English-Hindi”.Here I am trying to introduce you to the fundamentals of Marathi through English. But at some places, I have taken the help of examples from Hindi.
Hindi and Marathi both follow the same script i.e. Devanagari. The pronunciation of letters is the same. So those who can read-write Hindi, for them reading Marathi is easy. They will save their time learning pronunciation. So if you know Hindi, this journey to learn Marathi will be comparatively easy. But those who do not know Hindi need not feel upset, but they just need to put little more effort learning script and pronunciation.
My own experience says that reading the language from its own script helps a lot. So learn the Devanagari script first. So learning Marathi will be easier if you read it directly from Devanagari instead of Romanized. For most of the examples, transliteration is given in Roman script is as well. This is for the convenience of search-ability on the internet.

title: Which-That / What-That sentences
content: Which-That / What-That sentences
intro: WH-words (what, which, when) link condition and post-condition. Use WH-word synonyms or जो-तो pairs: जो-तो, जी-ती, जे-ते, जेव्हा-तेव्हा, जसा-तसा, etc.

blocks.heading: Using WH-words
blocks.content: Translate using WH-question synonyms: काय, कोणते, केव्हा etc.

blocks.heading: WH-word examples
rows: I could not understand what you just said
तू आत्ता काय म्हणालास ते मला कळले नाही
tU AttA kAy mhaNAlAs te malA kaLale nAhI
Which fruit you want, show me that
तुला कोणते फळ हवे आहे ते मला दाखव
tulA koNate phaL have Ahe te malA dAkhav

blocks.heading: Using जो-तो pairs
blocks.content: Use companion pairs: जो in condition, तो in post-condition. Same for जी-ती, जे-ते.

blocks.heading: Examples with जो-तो
rows: I could not understand what you just said
तू आत्ता जे म्हणालास ते मला कळले नाही
tU AttA je mhaNAlAs te malA kaLale nAhI
When you come I feel happy
तू जेव्हा येतोस तेव्हा मला आनंद वाटतो
tU jevhA yetos tevhA malA AnaMda vATato
What is in your mind, tell me that
तुझ्या मनात जे आहे, ते मला सांग
tujhyA manAt je Ahe, te malA sAMg
Which fruit you want, show me that
तुला जे फळ हवे आहे ते मला दाखव
tulA je phaL have Ahe te malA dAkhav
When you(girl) come I feel happy
तू जेव्हा येतेस तेव्हा मला आनंद वाटतो
tU jevhA yetes tevhA malA AnaMda vATato
Go back, the way you have come
तू जसा आलास तसा परत जा
tU jasA AlAs tasA parat jA

blocks.heading: With prepositions
blocks.content: Companion form changes with preposition: त्याला–ज्याला, त्याच्याबरोबर–ज्याच्याबरोबर, त्यांच्यासाठी–ज्यांच्यासाठी.

blocks.heading: Preposition examples
rows: Give it to him, who wants it
Tell me about them whom you read about
ज्यांच्याबद्दल तू वाचलेस त्यांच्याबद्दल मला सांग
jyAMchyAbaddal tU vAchales tyAMchyAbaddal malA sAMg

blocks.heading: Wherever, whenever, whichever
blocks.content: Repeat companion word: जे जे (whichever n.), जी जी (whichever f.), जिथे जिथे (wherever), जेव्हा जेव्हा (whenever).

blocks.heading: -ever examples
rows: Wherever you will go I will come
तू जिथे जिथे जाशील तिथे तिथे मी येईन
tU jithe jithe jAshIl tithe tithe mI yeIn
Whichever vegetables you want, you buy it
तुला जी जी भाजी हवी आहे ती ती तू विकत घे
tulA jI jI bhAjI havI Ahe tI tI tU vikat ghe
Whenever you call he will answer
तू जेव्हा जेव्हा फोन करशील तेव्हा तेव्हा तो उत्तर देईल
tU jevhA jevhA phon karashIl tevhA tevhA to uttar deIl

title: Working with nouns - Gender & Plurals in Marathi
content: Working with nouns - Gender & Plurals in Marathi
intro: In Marathi, non-living objects can be masculine or feminine. Gender is not rule-based. Plural forms depend on gender and ending vowel.

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content:
In English, non-living objects are neuter. In Marathi: आंबा (AMbA) (mango) is masculine, सिगरेट (sigareT) (cigarette) is feminine, पान (pAn) (leaf) is neuter. There are no specific rules for gender—learn as you go.

For words not matching rules, singular and plural are same (e.g. माठ - माठ (mATh - mATh)).

blocks.heading: Plural formation rules
rows: फळा - फळे, फुगा - फुगे (phaLA - phaLe, phugA - phuge)
बरणी - बरण्या, सावली - सावल्या (baraNI - baraNyA, sAvalI - sAvalyA)
माळ - माळा, मांजर - मांजरी (mAL - mALA, mAMjar - mAMjarI)

blocks.heading: Note
blocks.content: For Feminine ending with अ(a), two ways exist: plural with आ(A) or ई(I). No specific rule. For माठ (mATh) (earthen pot), plural is माठ (mATh) (same).

title: Working with nouns - Prepositions in Marathi
content: Working with nouns - Prepositions in Marathi
intro: Nouns have सामान्य रूप (sAmAnya rUp) for prepositions. Depends on gender and ending vowel. If not matching rules, सामान्य रूप = noun itself.

blocks.heading: To preposition
blocks.content:
ला (lA) for singular, ना (nA) for plural. To chick -> पिल्लाला (pillAlA). To chicken -> पिल्लांना (pillAMnA). To spear -> भाल्याला (bhAlyAlA). To spears -> भाल्यांना (bhAlyAMnA).

To his fort: किल्ला (killA) -> किल्ल्याला (killyAlA). त्याचा किल्ला (tyAchA killA) -> त्याच्या किल्ल्याला (tyAchyA killyAlA).

blocks.heading: Another style
blocks.content: Some use च्या (chyA) before every preposition: किल्ल्यासाठी (killyAsAThI) or किल्ल्याच्यासाठी (killyAchyAsAThI). Both correct; prefer first (without च्या).

blocks.heading: In/At
blocks.content: आत/त (At/t). With च्या (chyA) use आत (At). Without च्या append त (t). किल्ल्यात (killyAt) or किल्ल्याच्या आत (killyAchyA At). At home: घरात (gharAt) or घरी (gharI). आत = inside; ई = at. वर (var) sometimes used for "at" (e.g. airport, village well).
